claim: Is Segmental Ureterectomy Associated with Inferior Survival for Localized Upper-Tract Urothelial Carcinoma of the Ureter Compared to Radical Nephroureterectomy?
summary: The article investigates the overall survival (OS) of patients undergoing Segmental Ureterectomy (SU) compared to those undergoing Radical Nephroureterectomy (RNU) for localized upper-tract urothelial carcinoma. It concludes that SU is not associated with inferior survival compared to RNU.

section_name_map.Section 1.3: 2.3. Definition of the Variable of Interest
section_name_map.Section 2.1: 3.1. Baseline Patient Characteristics and Predictors of Receiving Segmental Ureterectomy

evidences.label: support
evidences.evidence_text: After adjusting for confounding, we found no difference in OS between patients undergoing SU compared to those who received RNU.
evidences.rationale: This statement supports the claim by indicating that there is no difference in overall survival between the two surgical approaches.

evidences.label: refute
evidences.rationale: This evidence suggests that the hazard ratio for SU indicates it does not confer inferior survival compared to RNU, thus refuting the claim.
